questions.question: A bank offers 5% compound interest calculated on half-yearly basis. A customer deposits Rs. 1600 each on 1st January and 1st July of a year. At the end of the year, the amount he would have gained by way of interest is:
questions.shortcut: Amount = P(1 + R/100)^T. CI = Amount - P
questions.trap_type: Forgetting that CI > SI for same P, R, T (except first year)

questions.question: The compound interest on Rs. 30,000 at 7% per annum is Rs. 4347. The period (in years) is:
questions.explanation:
Amount = Rs. (30000 + 4347) = Rs. 34347.
